lalita-sahasranama: Supreme hymn containing 1000 names of Goddess Lalita Tripurasundari
Bestows all four purusharthas through the Divine Mother: dharma, artha, kama, and moksha
Invokes the blessing of beauty, grace, and magnetic personality on the reciter
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins, doshas, and karmic debts accumulated over lifetimes
Invokes deep wisdom, spiritual power, and kundalini awakening
Recitation fulfils all desires and leads to union with the Divine Mother
lalita-trishati: Contains 300 sacred names of Goddess Lalita Tripurasundari
Grants the fourfold aims of life under Lalita's grace: dharma, artha, kama, and moksha
Invokes the blessing of beauty, grace, and spiritual magnetism
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of sins and karmic obstacles from past lives
Considered even more secret and powerful than Lalita Sahasranama
Taught by Lord Hayagriva to Sage Agastya as supreme devotion
lingashtakam: Traditionally recited for removal of sins accumulated over many lifetimes
Traditionally recited for removal of eight types of poverty (ashta daridra)
Honours the formless Shiva Linga as the source of all worship merit
Invokes the blessing of shivaloka after death
Suited to chanting during Shiva Linga abhishekam on Mondays
Traditionally recited for removal of pride, ego, and arrogance
madhurashtakam: Dwells on Krishna's sweetness of form, speech, and play to deepen love for him
Fills the heart with divine sweetness and joy
Traditionally recited for removal of mental stress and brings inner peace
Purifies the mind and elevates consciousness
Attracts divine grace and blessings of Sri Krishna
Helps in developing Bhakti Rasa (devotional sentiment)
maha-shivratri-stotram: Specifically meant for Maha Shivaratri night worship
Guides the devotee through the four-prahar puja vidhi
Washes away sins gathered across many lifetimes through Shivaratri vigil
Carries the devotee toward release from the cycle of birth and death
Pleases Lord Shiva when recited during night vigil
mahakal-stotram: Conquers the fear of death (Mrityunjaya aspect)
Equivalent to pilgrimage to Mahakaleshwar Jyotirlinga
Frees the reciter from accumulated sins and opens the path to liberation
Guards against untimely death and sudden accidents through Mahakal's grace
Invokes victory over time and all obstacles
Invokes the blessing of the fearless grace of bhairava
mahishasura-mardini-stotram-excerpt: Celebrates Durga's triumph over the buffalo-demon Mahishasura
Calls on Durga's victorious shakti to overcome adversaries and inner foes
Shields the devotee under the Mother's armour against danger and fear
Recited during Navratri and Durga Puja to earn the Mother's merit
Kindles courage and resolve before battles and difficult undertakings
mallikarjuna-stotram: Equivalent to pilgrimage to Srisailam, the second Jyotirlinga
Traditionally recited for removal of brahma-hatya and all major sins
Invokes fruit equal to one crore yajnas
Recited at Srisailam to gain the wishes the heart holds dear
Invokes the blessing of shivaloka after death
Clears obstacles on the spiritual path through Mallikarjuna's grace
mangal-sahasranama: Traditionally recited for removal of mangal dosha (kuja dosha) from horoscope
Traditionally invoked for removal of obstacles in marriage (mangalya dosha)
Invokes freedom from debts and enemies
Invokes the blessing of land, property, and real estate gains
Invokes courage, strength, and martial success
Six months of recitation grants all siddhis
mangal-stotram: Five-verse hymn pacifying the planet Mangal (Mars) on Tuesdays
Strengthens a weak or afflicted Mars to bestow vigour and drive
Guards the reciter against accidents, injury, and hostile forces
Eases Mangal dosha and brings harmony to married life
Kindles courage and bold resolve through Mangal's martial energy
mantra-pushpam: Vedic hymn from the Taittiriya Aranyaka extolling water as the source of all
Praises the cosmic waters and their hidden unity with the supreme
Chanted at the close of puja to crown worship with auspiciousness
Recited for abundance, rainfall, and unbroken prosperity
Concludes archana and aarti across temples and home worship
markandeya-stuti: Invokes victory over untimely death (akaal mrityu nivaran)
Invokes the blessing of long life and health
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of death completely
Protects children from dangers and illness
Steadies the heart with courage as it did for boy-sage Markandeya
matsya-stotram: Invokes the protective power of Vishnu's first avatar
Saves the devotee from danger and sin as Matsya saved Manu from the deluge
Traditionally invoked for protection from floods, water-related dangers, and natural calamities
Invokes knowledge and wisdom as matsya taught the puranas
Recited to safeguard sacred knowledge as Matsya rescued the Vedas
Traditionally recited to invoke preservation of dharma in one's life
meera-bhajan-key-verses: Awakens the purest form of devotional love (Prema Bhakti)
Invokes fearlessness in the face of worldly opposition
Cleanses the heart through Meera's verses of longing for Giridhar
Traditionally associated with the constant presence of krishna in consciousness
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of forms of worldly attachment
Invokes the blessing of the grace of giridhar gopal on the devotee
Inspires complete surrender and selfless devotion
nageshwar-stotram: Equivalent to pilgrimage to Nageshwar, the tenth Jyotirlinga
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of snakes and poison-related afflictions
Associated in tradition with diseases including tumors and chronic ailments
Dissolves long-held sins through Nageshwar's protective grace
Invokes the blessing of complete happiness and spiritual peace
Leads the devotee toward shiva-sayujya, union with Shiva
narasimha-ashtottara-shatanamavali: Ideal for archana (worship) with flowers or kumkum offering 108 names
Invokes powerful protection of lord narasimha
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of fears, diseases, and enemies
Invokes the blessing of courage, strength, and divine grace
Bears special potency when chanted on Narasimha Jayanti
narmada-stotram: Hymn to the holy river Narmada, born of Shiva's penance
Praises the Narmada whose every pebble is revered as a Shiva Linga
Mere sight of the Narmada is held to cleanse, as this hymn affirms
Recited to wash away sins that even other rivers cannot dissolve
Chanted on Mondays and Maha Shivaratri along the Narmada's banks
navagraha-kavacham: Protective armour invoking all nine planets for graha shanti
Seeks the favour of Surya, Chandra, and the seven grahas together
Shields the wearer from malefic planetary periods and unseen harm
Recited to soften afflicted dashas and restore planetary balance
Ideal for Navagraha graha shanti and worship
navagraha-stotram-brihaspati: Verse honouring Brihaspati (Jupiter), guru of the celestials
Strengthens a weak Guru graha for wisdom and good counsel
Recited to clear obstacles to learning, marriage, and progeny
Bestows knowledge, wealth, and sound judgement through Jupiter's grace
Chanted on Thursdays and Guru Purnima for the planet's blessing
neelakanthesha-stotram: Traditionally invoked for protection from all forms of poison and toxicity
Traditionally recited for removal of diseases and grants health
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins through shiva's infinite compassion
Teaches the supreme sacrifice of Lord Shiva for the world
Powerful remedy for Nag Panchami and Maha Shivaratri
Recalls Shiva swallowing the halahala to dispel fear and danger
padmanabha-ashtakam: Eight verses adoring Vishnu as Padmanabha reclining on Ananta
Frees the reciter from sins and turns the heart toward liberation
Traditionally associated with peace, prosperity, and divine grace
Especially powerful for devotees visiting Padmanabhaswamy Temple
panchamukhi-hanuman-kavacham: Five-faced Hanuman armour from the Parasara Samhita for total protection
Calls the five faces of Hanuman to guard from every direction
Wards off black magic, evil eye, and unseen malefic forces
Recited to banish fear, nightmares, and hostile energies
Worn as protective kavacham on Tuesdays and Saturdays
parashurama-stotram: Invokes victory over enemies and immense strength
Invokes the warrior-sage power of the sixth avatar
Traditionally recited for removal of injustice and establishes dharma
Invokes the blessing of mastery of knowledge and martial arts
Leads the devout toward Vishnu's abode after a life of dharma
Protects Brahmins and upholds sacred traditions
parvati-ashtakam: Eight beautiful verses praising Goddess Parvati in all her aspects
Invokes Parvati as Sati, Durga, Chandi, Chamunda and Shiva simultaneously
Bestows lasting marital harmony and the Mother's serene grace
Blesses married women with strength of pativrata dharma
Ideal for Monday and Friday worship of the Divine Mother
radha-stotram: Invokes krishna-prema (divine love for lord krishna)
Invokes the blessing of the supreme abode of radha-madhav
Connects the devotee with Radha's ahladini shakti (bliss power)
Deepens devotional sentiment and purifies the heart
Bestows mental calm and quiet joy through Radha's tender grace
Invokes the grace and protection of shri radha rani
